events.title: Explorers: Mondays
events.description:
(Ages 6-10)

Our Explorers program is designed for young, new climbers looking to gain confidence and hone their skills on the wall. This program meets once weekly and incorporates climbing games, activities, and technique instruction to build a solid climbing foundation.

events.title: Adventurers (Spring)
events.description:
(Ages 10+)

Our Adventurers program involves twice weekly sessions centered around developing climbing technique, athletic skill, and sportsmanship. Climbers will gain independence in a positive, collaborative environment while having fun and exploring movement.

events.title: Homeschool and High School Hours
events.description:
Homeschool and High School Hours – Every Wednesday at Mosaic! Looking for something fun to do? Come hang out, make new friends, and climb with us at Mosaic Climbing every Wednesday for just $15!

If you're a high school student (ages 14–18) from any school or a local homeschool student, stop by and purchase your discounted Day Pass between 12:00 and 4:00 PM. Whether you’re new to climbing or a seasoned pro, everyone’s welcome. You must check in by 4 pm.

Bring your friends, challenge yourself, and be part of an awesome climbing community!

events.title: Creative Wellness Massage Pop-Up
events.description: Creative Wellness Massage is a local, clinically focused bodywork practice that works with people who move—and want to keep moving without pain.They specialize in pain management, sports and overuse injuries, as well as mobility and assisted stretching. They strive to help active adults recover smarter, not harder.

events.title: Top Rope Class
events.description: Want to start learning the ropes? Sign up for our top rope belaying class. Nonmembers must purchase a day pass as well.

events.title: Learn to Lead
events.description: Our lead clinic includes 6 hours of instruction split into a two part series. The first session of the clinic consists of a lot of verbal instruction including proper clipping techniques, how to manage the rope in different situations, and how to properly catch a fall. At the end of the first day, we practice mock leading on the auto belays with proper clipping technique and rope management. There is a week between the 2 sessions where we encourage climbers to practice the skills they have learned including mock leading in the facility. The second session is more practical application of the skills discussed during the first day. We will practice catching our partner at different heights on the wall and how to give a soft catch.

events.title: Summer Climbing Club
events.description: Mosaic’s youth summer climbing club introduces children to climbing and helps them refine their climbing technique through skill based activities and challenges. Our youth programs keep kids moving while they collaborate to solve problems and tackle obstacles. Appropriate for children 6 - 13 years of age
